meta.title: San Martin's Bridge — Toledo, Spain
meta.description: Complete guide to San Martin's Bridge: 14th-century pedestrian bridge with spectacular Tagus River views, Gothic architecture and medieval history.

hero.title: San Martin's Bridge
hero.subtitle: 14th-century pedestrian bridge with sweeping views of the Tagus River

about.title: Discover the Bridge
about.description: San Martin's Bridge, built in the 14th century, is a medieval architectural jewel spanning the Tagus River in Toledo. This pedestrian bridge offers spectacular views of the walled city and the natural Tagus landscape, serving as a living testament to Spanish history.

highlights.title: Highlights
items: Panoramic views of the Tagus River and Toledo city
Ideal photography spot to capture Toledo's essence
Historic connection between Tagus River banks

items.title: Medieval Architecture
items.description: Admire the impressive Gothic architecture of the bridge with its five arches of different sizes. The medieval construction demonstrates the mastery of 14th-century architects and their ability to adapt to the rocky Tagus terrain.

items.title: Panoramic Tagus River Views
items.description: From the bridge you can enjoy the best views of the Tagus River and its meander that surrounds the city of Toledo. The natural landscape perfectly combines with the historic architecture of the city.

items.title: Photography and Viewpoints
items.description: San Martin's Bridge is an ideal spot for photography, offering multiple angles to capture Toledo's beauty. Sunrise and sunset provide perfect lighting for spectacular photos.

items.title: Connection with History
items.description: Walking across this bridge transports you to medieval times. It has witnessed centuries of Toledan history and continues to be a vital element of the city's cultural heritage.

gallery.subtitle: Images of the bridge and surroundings

images.caption: Medieval bridge view

reviews.title: Real Visitor Reviews
reviews.declaration: Ratings and reviews from Google Maps (last updated: 2026). We show only a selection of verified high-rated reviews. To see all complete and most recent reviews, click the link below.
reviews.seeAll: See all reviews on Google Maps

items.name: Ana Martínez
items.text: Impressive medieval bridge with spectacular views of the Tagus River. Perfect for photos and peaceful walks. The Gothic architecture is stunning.

items.name: Javier Rodríguez
items.text: A historical gem in Toledo. The views from the bridge are magnificent, especially at sunset. Well worth the visit.

items.name: Laura Fernández
items.text: Beautiful 14th-century bridge. Walking across it transports you to medieval times. The views of the Tagus and city are unforgettable.

items.name: Manuel García
items.text: Excellent example of medieval architecture. The perfect place to contemplate Toledo's natural beauty and the Tagus River. Highly recommended.

items.name: Carmen López
items.text: Toledo wouldn't be the same without this bridge. The panoramic views are spectacular and the history it holds is fascinating.

items.name: Roberto Sánchez
items.text: Very well-preserved medieval bridge. Perfect for photography and enjoying Tagus views. A must-visit in Toledo.

items.name: Isabel Gómez
items.text: Wonderful testament to medieval engineering. The natural Tagus setting makes it even more special. Don't miss it.

items.name: Francisco Pérez
items.text: The views from the bridge are stunning. The combination of history, architecture, and nature makes it a unique place.

hours.label: Opening Hours
hours.value: Open 24 hours — Free pedestrian access. Best time to visit is sunrise or sunset for the best photography.

transport.label: Getting There
transport.value: From Madrid: 45 min by high-speed train or 1 hour by car. From Toledo station: 20 min walk or 10 min by bus.

parking.label: Parking
parking.value: Free parking available nearby. In peak season (September-May) it can be limited — arrive early recommended.

accessibility.label: Accessibility
accessibility.value: Pedestrian access with ramps available. Bridge has uneven stone surface — comfortable footwear recommended.

sections.title: Third-Party Services
